text: In addition, the spleen and bone marrow, serving as key reservoirs for inflammatory cells, prove instrumental in driving the development of SAP. These two organs form a vicious cycle through cell migration, cytokine interactions, and metabolic reprogramming, collectively driving SAP toward multiorgan failure. ‚Äã‚ÄãTo elucidate the impact of Ab-Fc-EL-EV on bone marrow mobilization and the splenic inflammatory cell reservoir, we conducted comprehensive flow cytometry analysis of major immune cell populations in the spleen and bone marrow. ‚Äã‚ÄãIn the bone marrow, no significant differences were observed among groups for CD4+ T cells, CD8Œ±+ T cells, Ly6Chigh monocytes, or Ly6Clow monocytes, indicating that neither SAP modeling nor any therapeutic intervention affected the production or mobilization of these immune cells. In contrast, SAP modeling significantly increased the proportions of macrophages and neutrophils in the bone marrow. All treatments‚ÄîQue anti-Prdx1 antibody, Ab-Fc-EV@Que, and Ab-Fc-EL-EV@Que formulation‚Äîeffectively reduced macrophage and neutrophil levels. Notably, Ab-Fc-EL-EV@Que did not exhibit superior efficacy to other treatments, suggesting its therapeutic optimization primarily targets pancreatic pathology rather than systemic bone marrow modulation (Figure S5A and S5B). Regarding splenic immune cells, SAP modeling significantly increased the proportion of CD4+ T cells but not CD8Œ±+ T cells. All treatments‚ÄîQue, anti-Prdx1 antibody, Ab-Fc-EV@Que, and Ab-Fc-EL-EV@Que formulation‚Äîeffectively reduced the CD4+ T cell proportion, with no significant differences observed among therapeutic groups. This suggests that the primary therapeutic target of Ab-Fc-EL-EV@Que is not localized to the spleen.‚Äã‚Äã Similarly, ‚Äã‚ÄãLy6Chigh monocytes‚Äã‚Äã were significantly elevated in SAP models. While all treatments reduced their proportion, ‚Äã‚ÄãAb-Fc-EL-EV@Que exhibited the most significant efficacy‚Äã‚Äã. For ‚Äã‚Äãsplenic macrophages‚Äã‚Äã, SAP modeling induced a marked increase, but ‚Äã‚Äãonly Ab-Fc-EL-EV@Que significantly reduced their proportion‚Äã‚Äã. ‚Äã‚ÄãSplenic neutrophils‚Äã‚Äã were also elevated in SAP models. ‚Äã‚ÄãAnti-Prdx1 antibody, Ab-Fc-EV@Que, and Ab-Fc-EL-EV@Que‚Äã‚Äã effectively lowered neutrophil levels, whereas ‚Äã‚Äãmonotherapy with Que showed no significant effect‚Äã‚Äã (Figure S4C-D). These results indicate that ‚Äã‚ÄãAb-Fc-EL-EV@Que systematically regulates inflammation by suppressing macrophage recruitment, Ly6Chigh monocyte expansion, and potentially modulating adaptive immune cells (e.g., CD4+ T cells)‚Äã‚Äã. However, this systemic immunomodulation was ‚Äã‚Äãless pronounced than its local anti-inflammatory effects in the pancreas. This may arise because lack of tissue targeting: when treatment antibody is conjugated in EVs, Fc-EL-EVs lack specific homing to bone marrow and spleen; or systemic complexity‚Äã‚Äã: The dynamic interplay between SAP progression and therapeutic interventions involves multifaceted inflammatory cascades beyond single-organ targeting. All these findings suggested that Ab-Fc-EL-EV@Que could specifically alleviate systematic and local inflammatory responses in SAP, inhibiting extracellular Prdx1-mediated macrophage pro-inflammation response and enhancing antioxidant capacity of intracellular Prdx1 in acinar cells, along with minimized side effects in peripheral organs.
heading_context: 3.8 Regulatory mechanism exploration of Ab-Fc-EL-EV@Que against SAP
